SHA-256 for preimage hashing to stay compatible with Bitcoin HTLC scripts.      Swap existence is tracked with a single bool per swap for minimal storage cost.      All swap parameters must be supplied on redeem/refund and are verified via hash. ccak256":"0x982c5cb790ab941d1e04f807120a71709d4c313ba0bfc16006447ffbd27fbbd5","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://8150ceb4ac947e8a442b2a9c017e01e880b2be2dd958f1fa9bc405f4c5a86508","dweb:/ipfs/QmbcBmFX66AY6Kbhnd5gx7zpkgqnUafo43XnmayAM7zVdB"]},"lib/openzeppelin-contracts/contracts/utils/introspection/IERC165.sol":{"keccak256":"0x8891738ffe910f0cf2da09566928589bf5d63f4524dd734fd9cedbac3274dd5c","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://971f954442df5c2ef5b5ebf1eb245d7105d9fbacc7386ee5c796df1d45b21617","dweb:/ipfs/QmadRjHbkicwqwwh61raUEapaVEtaLMcYbQZWs9gUkgj3u"]},"src/HTLCErc20.sol":{"keccak256":"0xedb19c49317baf515d0304f9bb95a3d141655a76fa33b9603df63f7b885f764e","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://6f026b51f7bae6c019894a6d369544203c87a03e03955741ef0dbea0c1f519d3","dweb:/ipfs/QmVJFkTArPU4hLcCrAFfysxdoAR4vzeDiUZj89oBAqCu5r"]}},"version":1}

create(bytes32,uint256,address,address,a.details: Tokens are always pulled from msg.sender. The refundAddress param controls      who can call refund — useful for coordinators/routers acting on behalf of a user.

params.amount: Token amount to lock (caller must have approved this contract)
params.claimAddress: Address authorized to redeem the locked tokens
params.preimageHash: SHA-256 hash of the secret preimage
params.refundAddress: Address that can refund after timelock (does not have to be msg.sender)
params.timelock: Unix timestamp after which the refund address can reclaim tokens

create(bytes32,uint256,address,address,u.details: Convenience wrapper — uses msg.sender as the sender (refund address)

params.amount: Token amount to lock (caller must have approved this contract)
params.claimAddress: Address authorized to redeem the locked tokens
params.preimageHash: SHA-256 hash of the secret preimage — used as the swap identifier in events
params.timelock: Unix timestamp after which the sender can reclaim tokens

redeem(bytes32,uint256,address,address,u.details: Only the designated claimAddress can call this — msg.sender is used as      claimAddress in the key lookup. Tokens are sent to msg.sender.

params.preimage: Secret whose SHA-256 hash matches the preimageHash used at creation
params.timelock: Timelock that was set at creation

redeemBySig(bytes32,uint256,address,addr.details: Anyone can call this function. The claimAddress is recovered from the signature      which includes msg.sender as the authorized caller, destination, sweepToken,      minAmountOut, and callsHash. Tokens are sent to msg.sender (not claimAddress).      All execution parameters are cryptographically bound to the signature, so the      outcome is guaranteed by the signer regardless of who submits the transaction.

params.callsHash: Hash of the calls array (bound to signature, prevents call substitution)
params.destination: Address where the caller intends to route tokens after redeem
params.minAmountOut: Minimum amount of sweepToken required (bound to signature)
params.preimage: Secret whose SHA-256 hash matches the preimageHash used at creation
params.sweepToken: Token the caller will sweep to destination (bound to signature)
params.timelock: Timelock that was set at creation

returns.claimAddress: The address recovered from the signature

refund(bytes32,uint256,address,address,u.details: Convenience wrapper — tokens are sent back to msg.sender

params.claimAddress: Claim address that was set at creation
params.preimageHash: The preimage hash used at creation
params.timelock: Timelock that was set at creation

refund(bytes32,uint256,address,address,u.details: msg.sender must still be the original sender (enforced via the hash).      Useful for sending tokens to a coordinator/router for further processing.

params.claimAddress: Claim address that was set at creation
params.destination: Address to receive the refunded tokens
params.preimageHash: The preimage hash used at creation
params.timelock: Timelock that was set at creation

refundBySig(bytes32,uint256,address,addr.details: The claimAddress signs to waive the timelock, enabling collaborative refunds.      Anyone can call this function. The claimAddress is recovered from the signature.      Tokens are sent to msg.sender (not the refund address or claimAddress).      All execution parameters are cryptographically bound to the signature.

params.destination: Address where the caller intends to route tokens after refund
params.minAmountOut: Minimum amount of sweepToken required (bound to signature)
params.preimageHash: The preimage hash used at creation (NOT the preimage — refunder doesn't know it)
params.refundAddress: The original sender/refund address from HTLC creation
params.sweepToken: Token the caller will sweep to destination (bound to signature)
params.timelock: Timelock that was set at creation

returns.claimAddress: The address recovered from the signature

computeKey(bytes32,uint256,address,addre.notice: Compute the storage key for a swap from its parameters

create(bytes32,uint256,address,address,a.notice: Lock ERC20 tokens with an explicit refund address

create(bytes32,uint256,address,address,u.notice: Lock ERC20 tokens into a new hash time-locked swap

isActive(bytes32,uint256,address,address.notice: Check whether a swap with the given parameters is active

redeem(bytes32,uint256,address,address,u.notice: Redeem tokens by revealing the correct preimage (direct claim)

redeemBySig(bytes32,uint256,address,addr.notice: Redeem tokens using an EIP-712 signature from the claimAddress (gasless / delegated)

refund(bytes32,uint256,address,address,u.notice: Reclaim tokens after the timelock has expired
Reclaim tokens after the timelock has expired, sending to a specified destination

refundBySig(bytes32,uint256,address,addr.notice: Refund tokens before timelock using an EIP-712 signature from the claimAddress
